intended_use.medical_function: Healthcare appointment management system enabling patient consultations, clinical data recording, handover between practitioners, EHR integration (TakeCare), e-prescription management, and payment processing. Supports asynchronous, drop-in, and scheduled appointment workflows.
intended_use.use_environment: Cloud-based SaaS platform accessed via web browsers and mobile applications. Integrates with external systems: Directory (practitioner/patient data), MeetingPlace (video consultations), Notifier (push notifications), TakeCare EHR, e-prescription services, Klarna payment gateway.
intended_use.operating_principle: Spring Boot microservice with JPA persistence to MySQL/MariaDB, JMS messaging for event distribution, REST API for client access, role-based access control via JWT, transactional integrity via @Transactional annotations.

safety_characteristics.characteristic: Clinical data integrity - appointment notes, diagnoses, prescriptions must be accurately stored and retrieved without corruption or loss
safety_characteristics.limits: Data must persist through handovers, system restarts, and integration exports. Database constraints enforce referential integrity.

safety_characteristics.characteristic: Appointment ownership and responsibility tracking - system must maintain accurate record of which practitioner is responsible for a patient at any time
safety_characteristics.limits: State transitions must be atomic and logged. Handover process enforces UNIQUE constraint on appointment_id to prevent concurrent ownership claims.

safety_characteristics.characteristic: Authorization and access control - only authorized practitioners and patients can access sensitive medical records
safety_characteristics.limits: Care provider boundaries enforced unless explicit consent given. Cross-care-provider handovers require ENABLE_HANDOVERS_ACROSS_CARE_PROVIDERS feature flag.

safety_characteristics.characteristic: Notification delivery reliability - practitioners must be notified of new handovers, appointment changes, and critical events
safety_characteristics.limits: Dual-channel notification (JMS events + NotifierClient push notifications). Failures logged but may not block operation.

safety_characteristics.characteristic: Audit trail completeness - all clinical actions, handovers, and data access must be logged for medico-legal accountability
safety_characteristics.limits: appointment_handover_event table persists all status transitions. AuditLoggingService sends events to auditLogQueue asynchronously.

safety_characteristics.characteristic: Input validation constraints - handover parameters must be validated to prevent invalid states
safety_characteristics.limits: handoverCareUnitId varchar(50) required, handoverPractitionerRole varchar(50) required, handoverPractitionerId varchar(50) optional with DirectoryService validation, handoverNotes varchar(1024) max.

safety_characteristics.characteristic: Integration data consistency - clinical notes exported to TakeCare EHR must match internal records
safety_characteristics.limits: TakeCareNoteService performs XML sanitization and transformation. Failures logged in takecare_note_log_event table.

safety_characteristics.characteristic: Prescription safety - e-prescriptions must be accurately transmitted to external pharmacy systems
safety_characteristics.limits: Prescription entities persist medication details. External transmission handled by ExternalPrescriptionService with retry logic.

hazards.hazard: Notification delivery failure - practitioner not informed of pending handover
JMS event publication failure - external systems not synchronized with handover state
Database transaction failure during handover acceptance - partial state update
Invalid practitioner validation bypass - handover to non-existent or unauthorized practitioner
Cross-care-provider data exposure - patient data shared without consent
Concurrent handover race condition - multiple practitioners accept simultaneously
Handover to unavailable care unit - care unit no longer accepting patients or operational
Patient chat message delivery failure - patient unaware of practitioner change
Invalid state machine transition - appointment enters undefined handover state
Audit log persistence failure - no traceability of practitioner responsibility
Shift validation failure - practitioner accepts without active shift or valid credentials
Handover notes truncation - clinical context exceeds 1024 character limit
Unauthorized access to clinical data - authentication or authorization bypass
EHR export failure - clinical notes not transferred to TakeCare system
Prescription transmission error - e-prescription not received by pharmacy
Payment processing failure - patient charged incorrectly or billing data loss
Data sanitization bypass - malicious XML or script injection in clinical notes

hazardous_situations.situation: Practitioner proposes handover but target practitioner never receives notification, leaving appointment unattended
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Practitioner A initiates handover → NotifierClient.sendClinicNotification() fails due to network/service outage → Target practitioner B not aware → Appointment remains in PROPOSED state with no responder → Patient condition deteriorates without clinical attention
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Delayed diagnosis or treatment; patient deterioration; adverse clinical outcome
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Missing clinical oversight for acute patient could lead to hospitalization or permanent injury. System is used for time-sensitive consultations.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: NotifierClient uses redundant JMS events (appointmentEventSender.sendHandoverEvent) as secondary channel. Dual notification paths reduce likelihood. Network outages affecting both channels simultaneously are rare.

hazardous_situations.situation: Handover accepted in health-manager but JMS event not published, causing external systems to show wrong practitioner assignment
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Handover accepted → appointmentRepository.save() succeeds → appointmentEventSender.sendHandoverEvent() throws exception → External dashboards/reports show original practitioner → Duplicate care or missed care coordination
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Care coordination failure; duplicate treatments; conflicting clinical decisions
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: External system inconsistency creates operational confusion but appointment state in health-manager is authoritative. Logging provides audit trail for reconciliation.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: JMS infrastructure is highly reliable. Info-level logging (SWR-3.5) provides secondary audit mechanism. Transaction boundary ensures DB write succeeds first.

hazardous_situations.situation: Database transaction fails during handover acceptance after state change, corrupting appointment ownership
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: acceptHandover() called → handoverProcess fields updated → appointmentRepository.save() throws SQLException → Partial update persisted → Both practitioners believe they own appointment OR appointment orphaned
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: No practitioner takes responsibility; patient abandoned; severe delay in care
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Loss of practitioner accountability could result in complete absence of care for urgent patient, leading to life-threatening delay.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: @Transactional annotation ensures atomicity - either all changes commit or all rollback. UNIQUE constraint on appointment_id prevents concurrent accepts. Database ACID guarantees prevent partial writes.

hazardous_situations.situation: DirectoryService.isValidPractitioner() returns false positive, allowing handover to non-existent or inactive practitioner
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Handover proposed to practitionerId 'abc123' → DirectoryService incorrectly validates → Handover accepted by system → No actual practitioner receives assignment → Appointment orphaned
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Patient encounter with no responsible clinician; complete absence of medical care
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Patient scheduled for care but no practitioner exists to provide it. Could lead to severe adverse outcomes for acute conditions.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: DirectoryService is external authoritative source. Validation performed at propose AND accept stages. Error message 'cannot be handed over to an invalid practitioner %s' provides user feedback (SWR-3.1).

hazardous_situations.situation: ENABLE_HANDOVERS_ACROSS_CARE_PROVIDERS check bypassed, exposing patient data to unauthorized care provider without consent
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Feature flag check fails or misconfigured → Handover crosses care provider boundary → Patient medical records visible to Organization B without consent → Privacy breach; GDPR violation
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Privacy violation; regulatory penalties; loss of patient trust; legal liability
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Privacy breach is serious but does not directly cause physical harm. Regulatory and reputational consequences significant.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: Multiple checks: CustomizationService.getBooleanForCareUnit() validates flag, CareUnitRegistry.getCareUnit() validates same care provider (SWR-2.5), AppointmentAuthorizer checks consent. Error message 'cannot be handed over to careUnit at another careProvider' (SWR-3.1).

hazardous_situations.situation: Two practitioners simultaneously click 'Accept Handover', both database writes succeed, appointment assigned to both
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: PROPOSED handover visible to practitioners A and B → Both click accept at same moment → Race condition in transaction handling → Appointment.practitionerId set to both → Confusion about responsibility
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Duplicate care; conflicting treatments; wasted resources; potential clinical contradictions
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Creates operational confusion and potential for conflicting treatments, but unlikely to cause severe physical harm. Audit trail allows post-hoc resolution.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: UNIQUE constraint on appointment_id in appointment_handover_process (SWR-4.1) causes second transaction to fail. State machine only allows ACCEPT from PROPOSED state (SWR-1.3). @Transactional isolation prevents dirty reads.

hazardous_situations.situation: CareUnitRegistry returns stale or invalid data, handover directed to care unit no longer operational
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Care unit 'XYZ' closes for emergency → Registry not updated immediately → Handover proposed to 'XYZ' → Validation passes on stale data → No practitioners available at 'XYZ' → Appointment unattended
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Delayed access to care; patient deterioration while awaiting assignment
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Patient expecting care but transferred to non-operational unit. Delay could be hours, risking adverse outcomes for urgent conditions.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: CareUnitRegistry is actively maintained. Validation at propose checks careUnitRegistry.getCareUnit(id).isPresent() (SWR-2.5). Error message 'requires a valid careUnit and practitionerRole' (SWR-3.1).

hazardous_situations.situation: MeetingPlace chat service fails to deliver handover message, patient continues communicating with wrong practitioner
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Handover accepted → appointmentMeetingService.sendMessageToExternalMeeting() fails → Patient sends symptoms to old practitioner who no longer monitors → Messages missed → Symptom escalation not detected
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Missed clinical communications; delayed response to deteriorating symptoms; adverse outcome
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Communication breakdown creates risk but new practitioner has access to appointment and should proactively engage. UI typically shows practitioner change.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: MeetingPlace has high availability. Chat message sent on accept with patient visibility (SWR-2.3, SWR-3.3 'appointment_handover_accepted'). New practitioner added to meetings (addPractitionerToExternalMeeting, addPractitionerToInternalMeeting).

hazardous_situations.situation: Invalid state transition attempted (e.g., ACCEPTED → PROPOSED), corrupting handover process state
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Appointment in ACCEPTED state → Bug or malicious API call attempts PROPOSE → State validation fails but with unclear error → Appointment stuck in undefined state → Cannot complete or cancel handover
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Appointment workflow blocked; patient unable to receive care; administrative burden to manually resolve
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Operational disruption but patient can be rescheduled or manually reassigned. No immediate clinical harm.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: State machine strictly enforces transitions: ENABLED→PROPOSED→{ACCEPTED,DENIED,CANCELLED} (SWR-1.3). isClearForHandoverPropose() validation. Error messages 'is not clear for handover given practitioner status' and 'does not have an active handover process' (SWR-3.1).

hazardous_situations.situation: appointmentHandoverEventRepository.save() fails silently, no audit record created
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Handover accepted → logHandoverEvent() called → save() throws exception → Log warning but continue → No audit trail of who accepted responsibility → Medico-legal investigation cannot determine practitioner at time of incident
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Inability to investigate adverse events; compliance failure; legal liability for organization and practitioners
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Does not directly cause patient harm but creates legal/regulatory risk and impairs quality improvement investigations.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: Database persistence highly reliable. Info-level logging 'Appointment {} has changed handover status from: {} to: {}' provides secondary audit (SWR-3.5). appointment_handover_event table with FK to appointment ensures referential integrity (SWR-4.2).

hazardous_situations.situation: Practitioner accepts handover without valid active shift, taking responsibility without authorization
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Practitioner shift ends at 17:00 → Practitioner still logged in → Accepts handover at 17:05 → ShiftRepository returns null/stale shift → Practitioner may not be available → Patient care delayed
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Care continuity gap; practitioner not available when patient needs attention
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Creates service quality issue but backup on-call mechanisms typically exist. Unlikely to cause severe harm.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: AppointmentHelper.getPractitionerActiveShift() validates shift at accept time. Error message 'does not have permission to accept this appointment' (SWR-3.1). Shift validation is explicit requirement.

hazardous_situations.situation: Practitioner enters critical handover context >1024 characters, notes truncated, receiving practitioner missing vital information
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Patient has complex medication allergies → Practitioner types detailed handover notes 1200 chars → System truncates at 1024 → Allergy info at end of notes lost → Receiving practitioner prescribes contraindicated drug → Adverse drug reaction
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Treatment error due to incomplete information; adverse drug reaction; patient injury
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Missing context could lead to suboptimal decisions, but receiving practitioner has access to full medical record. Handover notes are supplementary, not primary source.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: varchar(1024) provides substantial space (SWR-1.1, SWR-4.6). UI likely enforces max length with counter. Practitioner can reference full appointment notes. Database constraint prevents silent truncation.

hazardous_situations.situation: JWT validation bypassed or authorization check fails, allowing unauthorized practitioner to view patient medical records
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Attacker obtains expired/forged JWT → AppointmentAuthorizer.hasReadAccessToAppointment() fails to reject → Sensitive patient data exposed → Privacy breach; GDPR violation; patient safety risk if attacker acts on data
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Privacy violation; potential for medical identity theft; compromised patient safety if data misused
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Unauthorized access to medical records is serious privacy violation with potential physical harm if data used maliciously (e.g., insurance fraud, blackmail).
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: Multi-layered authorization: JWT validation at controller level, AppointmentAuthorizer checks user.isPractitioner(), careUnit matching, consent checks (ACCESS_MEDICAL_RECORDS), cross-care-provider restrictions. Caching (@Cacheable) improves performance without compromising security.

hazardous_situations.situation: TakeCare EHR export fails, clinical documentation not integrated into patient's permanent medical record
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Consultation completed → Practitioner finalizes notes → TakeCareNoteService.exportNote() fails due to network/API error → Notes remain only in health-manager → Patient visits different provider → Medical history incomplete → Misdiagnosis or contraindicated treatment
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Fragmented medical record; clinical decisions made without complete history; potential treatment errors
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Creates risk of suboptimal care due to incomplete information, but receiving provider can request records. Not immediately life-threatening.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: TakeCareNoteService includes error handling and retry logic. takecare_note_log_event table logs failures for manual remediation. XML sanitization (NoteXmlSanitizer) prevents format errors. Integration monitoring typically alerts on failures.

hazardous_situations.situation: E-prescription transmission fails, patient arrives at pharmacy without valid prescription
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Practitioner prescribes medication → ExternalPrescriptionService.send() fails → Patient receives confirmation in app → Patient goes to pharmacy → Prescription not in system → Treatment delayed until manual prescription issued
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Delayed medication access; symptom progression; patient inconvenience and loss of trust
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Delay in medication access but not immediately life-threatening. Alternative prescribing methods available (paper prescription).
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: ExternalPrescriptionService includes retry logic and error handling. Prescription entity persists locally for audit. ExternalPrescriptionScheduler likely provides background retry. Monitoring alerts on transmission failures.

hazardous_situations.situation: Payment processing error leads to incorrect billing, patient charged for service not received or not charged for service provided
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Appointment completed → PaymentCreditService records charge → KlarnaClientV3 API fails → Payment state inconsistent → Patient disputes charge OR provider loses revenue → Financial and trust impact
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: Financial harm to patient or provider; administrative burden; loss of trust; potential service disruption if billing critical to operations
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Financial impact but no direct physical harm to patients. Billing errors can be corrected through customer service.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: Klarna is enterprise-grade payment processor with high reliability. PaymentCreditService likely includes transaction management. CreditEvent domain entity provides audit trail. Payment reconciliation processes detect discrepancies.

hazardous_situations.situation: Malicious XML or script injection in clinical notes bypasses sanitization, executed when rendered in UI or exported to EHR
hazardous_situations.foreseeable_sequence: Attacker practitioner enters notes with embedded script → NoteXmlSanitizer fails to strip malicious content → Notes exported to TakeCare → Script executed in EHR UI → Potential data exfiltration or system compromise
hazardous_situations.potential_harm: System compromise; data breach; integrity of clinical records compromised; potential safety impact if clinical data altered
hazardous_situations.severity_rationale: Security vulnerability could lead to data breach, but typically sandboxed from patient safety systems. Impact primarily confidentiality/integrity rather than availability.
hazardous_situations.probability_rationale: NoteXmlSanitizer explicitly designed to prevent injection attacks. Multiple layers: input validation, sanitization, output encoding. Spring Security provides additional XSS protection. Code review and security testing typically catch injection vulnerabilities.

risk_controls.control_description: Redundant notification channels: NotifierClient push notifications AND JMS event publication to appointmentHandoverEventTopic
Info-level logging records all handover status transitions with appointment ID, old status, new status, user role, and practitioner ID
@Transactional annotation ensures atomicity of handover acceptance - all database updates commit or rollback together
UNIQUE constraint on appointment_id in appointment_handover_process table prevents multiple concurrent handover processes
DirectoryService.isValidPractitioner() validation checks practitioner exists and is active before allowing handover
Error message displayed to user: 'Appointment [%s] at careUnit %s cannot be handed over to an invalid practitioner %s'
CustomizationService checks ENABLE_HANDOVERS_ACROSS_CARE_PROVIDERS feature flag before allowing cross-provider handovers
CareUnitRegistry validates that source and target care units belong to same care provider unless cross-provider enabled
Error message: 'Appointment [%s] at careUnit %s cannot be handed over to careUnit at another careProvider %s'
AppointmentAuthorizer checks consent (ACCESS_MEDICAL_RECORDS) for cross-care-provider access and only allows handover actions with flag enabled
UNIQUE constraint on appointment_id in appointment_handover_process causes database to reject second concurrent accept attempt
State machine validation: only PROPOSED state allows transition to ACCEPTED, preventing accepts from other states
CareUnitRegistry.getCareUnit(handoverCareUnitId).isPresent() validates care unit exists before allowing handover
Error message: 'Appointment [%s] at careUnit %s requires a valid careUnit and practitionerRole for handover'
HandoverMessage sent to patient chat via MeetingPlace on all handover state changes (PROPOSED, ACCEPTED, DENIED, CANCELLED)
Internationalized chat messages inform patient of handover: 'appointment_handover_accepted' visible in patient interface
State machine enforces valid transitions: ENABLED→PROPOSED→{ACCEPTED,DENIED,CANCELLED}. isClearForHandoverPropose() checks appointment status.
Error messages inform user of state violations: 'is not clear for handover given practitioner status', 'does not have an active handover process'
appointment_handover_event table persists all handover status changes with FK to appointment, timestamps, user, old/new status
Info-level logging provides secondary audit trail: 'Appointment {} has changed handover status from: {} to: {}. By {} {}. Current practitioner: {}'
AppointmentHelper.getPractitionerActiveShift() validates practitioner has active shift before allowing handover acceptance
Error message: 'does not have permission to accept this appointment' when shift validation fails

files: error handling in shift validation (inferred from SWR-3.1)

risk_controls.control_description: Database column handover_notes varchar(1024) enforces maximum length, preventing unbounded input

files: database schema DDL (inferred from SWR-1.1, SWR-4.6)

risk_controls.control_description: AppointmentAuthorizer.hasReadAccessToAppointment() enforces JWT validation, role checks, care unit matching, and consent verification
Care provider boundary enforcement: practitioners can only access appointments within same care provider unless explicit consent given
TakeCareNoteService includes error handling and retry logic for EHR export operations
takecare_note_log_event table logs all export attempts with success/failure status for manual remediation
NoteXmlSanitizer prevents format errors that could cause EHR export failures
ExternalPrescriptionService includes retry logic for failed prescription transmissions
Prescription entity persists locally, providing audit trail and basis for manual retry if automated transmission fails
PaymentCreditService transaction management ensures billing state consistency
CreditEvent domain entity provides audit trail of all payment transactions for reconciliation
NoteXmlSanitizer strips malicious content from clinical notes before storage and export
AuditLoggingService logs practitioner access to patient appointments for regulatory compliance and forensic investigation

residual_risks.residual_description: If both NotifierClient and JMS messaging fail simultaneously (e.g., infrastructure outage), target practitioner may not be notified. Requires manual follow-up or on-call system escalation.
residual_risks.disclosure_text: In rare cases of system infrastructure failure, handover notifications may be delayed. Care units should maintain backup communication channels (phone, pager) for urgent handovers.

residual_risks.residual_description: If DirectoryService data is corrupted or stale, validation may incorrectly pass. Requires periodic validation of DirectoryService data quality and practitioner roster accuracy.

If care unit becomes unavailable immediately after validation passes but before handover completes, appointment may be assigned to non-operational unit. Requires monitoring and alerting on care unit availability.

Critical information entered beyond 1024 character limit will be truncated. Practitioners must be trained to prioritize critical information at beginning of handover notes and reference full clinical record for details.
residual_risks.disclosure_text: Handover notes are limited to 1024 characters. For complex cases, practitioners should prioritize critical safety information and reference the full clinical record in the appointment notes.

residual_risks.residual_description: If TakeCare EHR system is offline for extended period, clinical notes may not be exported. Requires monitoring of export failures and manual export procedures for critical clinical documentation.
residual_risks.disclosure_text: Clinical notes are exported to external EHR systems. In case of integration failures, notes remain accessible within the appointment management system and can be manually exported.

residual_risks.residual_description: If e-prescription service is unavailable, prescriptions may not reach pharmacy. Requires fallback to manual prescribing methods (paper prescription, phone) until service restored.
residual_risks.disclosure_text: E-prescriptions are transmitted electronically. In rare cases of system unavailability, practitioners may issue paper prescriptions or contact the pharmacy directly.

gaps.description: Secondary logging audit trail (RC-002, RC-020) not verified through automated testing. Manual log review required to confirm logging behavior.
gaps.recommended_action: Add integration tests that verify log statements are written with correct format and content. Use log capturing framework (e.g., Logback test appender) to assert on log messages.

gaps.description: TakeCare EHR export controls (RC-026, RC-027, RC-028) lack automated verification. Integration with TakeCare is critical for continuity of care.
gaps.recommended_action: Implement integration tests with TakeCare test environment. Add contract tests to verify XML format compatibility. Monitor export success rate in production.

gaps.description: Prescription transmission controls (RC-029, RC-030) not verified. E-prescription is safety-critical for medication management.
gaps.recommended_action: Add integration tests with e-prescription service test environment. Implement monitoring and alerting for prescription transmission failures. Define SLA for retry attempts.

gaps.description: Payment processing controls (RC-031, RC-032) lack verification. Financial integrity is important for operational sustainability.
gaps.recommended_action: Implement integration tests with Klarna test environment. Add reconciliation tests to verify CreditEvent audit trail matches payment processor records. Implement daily financial reconciliation reports.

gaps.description: XML sanitization control (RC-033) not verified through automated testing. Injection attacks could compromise data integrity.
gaps.recommended_action: Add security unit tests with known malicious payloads (OWASP XSS vectors, XXE attacks, SQL injection strings). Perform penetration testing on note creation endpoints.

gaps.description: Audit logging for appointment access (RC-034) not verified through automated testing. Required for GDPR and regulatory compliance.
gaps.recommended_action: Add integration tests verifying AuditLogDto messages are sent to auditLogQueue with correct structure. Verify audit log completeness in production through sampling.

gaps.description: No verification that UNIQUE constraint on appointment_id is enforced under concurrent load. Race conditions could bypass database-level control.
gaps.recommended_action: Add load test simulating concurrent handover accepts from multiple practitioners. Verify database returns constraint violation error and transaction rollback occurs correctly.

gaps.description: No documentation of operational procedures for handling notification failures, EHR export failures, or prescription transmission failures.
gaps.recommended_action: Create standard operating procedures (SOPs) for: (1) Manual handover notification when system fails, (2) Manual EHR export procedures, (3) Manual prescription issuance process. Train clinical staff on fallback procedures.
